zh.Tyrley, Hales, Staffordshire, England: 英國英格蘭斯塔福爾泰利
zh.Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England: 英國格盧斯特斯爾素德勒
zh.Powick, Worcestershire, England: 英國武斯特斯爾波維其可
zh.lifeSummary: 當Alice de Beauchamp於1366年出生在英國武斯特斯爾波維其可時，她的父親Sir John de Beauchamp37歲，母親Joan de Cherlelton32歲。她於1385年7月18日之前與Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley在英國瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1443年2月8日在英國薩默塞特享年77歲。

zh-hans.Sudeley, Gloucestershire, England: 英国格盧斯特斯爾素德勒
zh-hans.Powick, Worcestershire, England: 英国武斯特斯爾波維其可
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice de Beauchamp于1366年出生在英国武斯特斯爾波維其可时，她的父亲Sir John de Beauchamp37岁，母亲Joan de Cherlelton32岁。她于1385年7月18日之前与Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley在英国瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1443年2月8日在英国薩默塞特享年77岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Beachum
meanings: Altered form of English   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: English:
possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

variants.name: Alys
meanings: Variant spelling of   Alice .

variants.name: Alis
meanings: equivalent of Alice .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice de Beauchamp was born in 1366, in Powick, Worcestershire, England, her father, Sir John de Beauchamp, was 37 and her mother, Joan de Cherlelton, was 32. She married Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley before 18 July 1385, in Warwickshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 8 February 1443, in Somerset, England, at the age of 77.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice de Beauchamp est née en 1366, à Powick, Worcestershire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Beauchamp, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Joan de Cherlelton, avait 32 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley avant 18 juillet 1385, à Warwickshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 février 1443, à Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 77 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice de Beauchamp 1366 in Powick, Worcestershire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Beauchamp 37 und ihre Mutter Joan de Cherlelton 32. Sie hat vor 18. Juli 1385 in Warwickshire, England Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Februar 1443 im Alter von 77 Jahren in Somerset, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Beauchamp è nata il nel 1366 nel Powick, Worcestershire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Beauchamp, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Joan de Cherlelton, aveva 32 anni. Lei sposato Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley prima del 18 luglio 1385, nel Warwickshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 8 febbraio 1443, nel Somerset, England, all'età di 77 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice de Beauchampが1366年にイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアポウィックで生まれた時，父Sir John de Beauchampは37歳，母Joan de Cherleltonは32歳であった。1385年7月18日以前にイギリスワーウィクシュアイアでThomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1443年2月8日にイギリスソメルセトで77歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice de Beauchamp(이)가 1366년 영국 워스스터실르 파윅에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Beauchamp(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan de Cherlelton(은)는 32세였다. 그녀는 1385년 7월 18일 이전 영국 워윅실르에서 Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1443년 2월 8일 영국 서머세트에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

mn.Tyrley, Hales, Staffordshire, England: Tyrley, Stæffordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice de Beauchamp nasceu em 1366, em Powick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Beauchamp, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Joan de Cherlelton, tinha 32 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley antes 18 de julho de 1385, em Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de fevereiro de 1443, em Somerset, Inglaterra, com 77 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice de Beauchamp родилась в 1366 г. в местности поуикк, ворсестиршир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Beauchamp было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan de Cherlelton, 32. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley перед 18 июля 1385 г. в местности варвиккшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 8 февраля 1443 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice de Beauchamp nació en 1366, en Powick, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Beauchamp, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Joan de Cherlelton, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Thomas le Boteler 4th Baron Sudeley antes de 18 de julio de 1385, en Warwickshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 8 de febrero de 1443, en Somerset, Inglaterra, a la edad de 77 años.
